---
title: "Miquel Sirera"
pagetitle: "Miquel Sirera Perelló"
description-meta: "PhD student at Northeastern · LLMs, distributed systems, edge AI."
toc: true
toc-location: right
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <meta property="og:title" content="Miquel Sirera Perelló">
        <meta property="og:description" content="PhD student at Northeastern building distributed LLMs that survive node failures, run on the edge, and stay accurate.">
        <meta property="og:image" content="files/headshot_new.jpg">
---

```{=html}
<section class="hero">
  <div class="hero-photo">
    <img src="files/headshot_new.jpg" alt="Miquel Sirera">
  </div>
  <div class="hero-body">
    <h1 class="hero-name">Miquel<br>Sirera&nbsp;Perelló</h1>
    <p class="hero-tagline">
      PhD student at <strong>Northeastern</strong> building
      <strong>distributed LLMs</strong> that survive node failures,
      run on the edge, and stay accurate under real‑world constraints.
    </p>
    <div class="hero-links">
      <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/mqsirera/" class="hero-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
        <i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i><span>LinkedIn</span>
      </a>
      <a href="https://github.com/mqsirera" class="hero-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
        <i class="bi bi-github"></i><span>GitHub</span>
      </a>
      <a href="mailto:sirera.m@northeastern.edu" class="hero-link">
        <i class="bi bi-envelope"></i><span>Email</span>
      </a>
      <a href="./files/MiquelSirera_AcademicCV_Sep2025.pdf" class="hero-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
        <i class="bi bi-filetype-pdf"></i><span>CV</span>
      </a>
      <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=DF9KcekAAAAJ" class="hero-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
        <i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i><span>Scholar</span>
      </a>
      <a href="https://www.instagram.com/mq.sirera/" class="hero-link" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
        <i class="bi bi-instagram"></i><span>Instagram</span>
      </a>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

::: {.intro-eyebrow}
[]{.dot} Open to collaboration · Boston, MA
:::

## Hi, I'm Miquel  {.unlisted}

I work at the intersection of **machine learning** and **distributed systems** — building large language models that can train and run across many devices, gracefully handle failure, and stay efficient at the edge.

Before Boston, I studied Data Science at UPC in Barcelona. I like research that ships, collaborators who push my thinking, and problems that don't have a clean answer yet. If any of what's below sounds interesting, [reach out](mailto:sirera.m@northeastern.edu) — I'm always up for a conversation.

::: {.chips}
[Distributed LLMs]{.chip .is-primary}
[Resilient Inference]{.chip}
[Edge AI]{.chip}
[Knowledge Editing]{.chip}
[Wireless + ML]{.chip}
[PyTorch]{.chip}
[CUDA]{.chip}
[Python]{.chip}
:::

::: {.section-eyebrow}
What I'm working on
:::

## Current projects

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
[Flagship]{.card-tag}

### Resilience for Distributed LLMs

Extending **JARVIS** — a framework that splits LLM layers across edge devices with limited compute. We add peer-to-peer recovery and layer redundancy so the system keeps serving when nodes fail. Tested on Gemma-2B across 18 software-defined radios in the NSF Colosseum emulator and a 7-node Raspberry Pi cluster. More in [Research](./research.qmd).
:::

::: {.project-card}
[Networking + ML]{.card-tag}

### Communication-aware DNN pruning

Training networks for distributed deployment so they need less inter-device communication while staying accurate. Pruning and placing neurons in CNNs and MLPs so they run well on the edge under mixed network conditions. Builds on [prior work from our group](https://ece.northeastern.edu/fac-ece/ioannidis/static/pdf/2023/C_Jian_CommAwarePrune_Infocom2023.pdf).
:::

::: {.project-card}
[LLMs]{.card-tag}

### Knowledge editing in LLMs

Looking at more efficient ways to update or correct specific facts and behaviors inside large language models — without full retraining, and without breaking everything else the model knows.
:::

:::

::: {.section-eyebrow}
A bit more
:::

## Background

::: {.timeline}

::: {.timeline-item}
[2024 — Present]{.ti-date}

#### PhD, Computer Engineering  {.ti-title .unlisted}

[Northeastern University · Boston]{.ti-place}

[Distributed LLMs, edge AI, and resilient inference. Advised in the Genesys lab.]{.ti-body}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[2023]{.ti-date}

#### Undergraduate Research Assistant  {.ti-title .unlisted}

[Genesys Lab, Northeastern · Boston]{.ti-place}

[Bachelor's thesis on AI for wireless — signal classification with deep models. Hooked me on AI + wireless and started my path here.]{.ti-body}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[2022]{.ti-date}

#### AI Engineer Intern  {.ti-title .unlisted}

[Abi Global Health · Remote]{.ti-place}

[MLOps monitoring and ML model work for a global tele-health platform.]{.ti-body}
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
[2019 — 2023]{.ti-date}

#### BSc, Data Science & Engineering  {.ti-title .unlisted}

[Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya · Barcelona]{.ti-place}

[GPA 8.89/10. Honors in Discrete Math & Logic, Mathematical Optimization, Search & Analysis of Information, and Advanced Topics of Data Science.]{.ti-body}
:::

:::

::: {.section-eyebrow}
Get in touch
:::

## Say hi

Best way to reach me is **[sirera.m@northeastern.edu](mailto:sirera.m@northeastern.edu)** or [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mqsirera/). I read everything, even if my replies aren't always fast.
